full_prompt: Design an aluminum subframe that mounts a moving payload onto two parallel size-35 ball-rail runner blocks (flanged, full-length FNS-equivalent), carrying a combined load at a cantilevered arm, and that passes the catalog load-moment envelope. The representative 2024 catalog values for the size-35 full-length ball-rail runner block are: dynamic load rating C = 59.6 kN, static load rating C0 = 89.5 kN, static moment capacities Mx = 1.12 kN*m, My = 1.12 kN*m, Mz = 1.21 kN*m. The subframe is a single aluminum plate (EN AW-6082-T6, yield 260 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, Young's modulus 70 GPa, Poisson 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3) measuring 400 mm (along rail travel) x 250 mm (across rails) x 30 mm thick nominal. It is bolted to four runner blocks (two per rail) using M8 grade 8.8 socket-head screws on the runner's catalog 72 x 40 mm mounting hole pattern (4 holes per runner, total 16 bolts). The payload attaches to the top face via a vertical arm whose tip is 150 mm above the subframe upper surface and 75 mm forward of the rail centerline midplane. The two rails run parallel at a center-to-center distance of 200 mm. Service load at the payload tip: vertical 3,000 N downward, horizontal (cross-rail) 1,500 N, and a dynamic duty cycle that develops accelerations up to 5 m/s^2 in the travel direction (inertial load 5 * payload + subframe mass). Each runner block sees a share of vertical force, cross-force, and rolling/pitching/yawing moments that must be computed by statics. Pass/fail: (R1) equivalent per-runner dynamic load P <= 0.5 * C = 29.8 kN for an assumed L10h life >= 10,000 hours at 0.5 m/s mean speed (rounded catalog practice); (R2) max per-runner static load F0 <= C0 / 4 = 22.4 kN with catalog static safety factor S0 >= 4 for mechanical-automation service; (R3) combined moment utilization (Mx/Mx_max) + (My/My_max) + (Mz/Mz_max) + (F0/C0) <= 1.0 per catalog combined-load interaction formula; (R4) subframe aluminum max von Mises stress <= 260 / 1.5 = 173 MPa (SF 1.5 on yield for machine frame service); (R5) subframe deflection at the payload attach point under service load <= 0.1 mm (positioning stiffness typical for size-35 ball-rail applications); (R6) first natural frequency of the loaded subframe >= 80 Hz to avoid resonance with typical servo bandwidth (nominally 20 Hz), i.e. isolation ratio >= 4. The subframe must be machined from a single billet with standard flatness IT8 across the rail mounting surface and parallelism 0.05 mm between the two rail reference surfaces. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the subframe; separate assembly file including placeholder runner-block envelopes; report with per-runner load, combined interaction utilization, max aluminum stress, payload-tip deflection, and first-mode natural frequency.
short_prompt:
Design an aluminum subframe that mounts a moving payload on two parallel size-35 ball-rail runner blocks (flanged, full-length FNS-equivalent), carrying a cantilevered load through the catalog load-moment envelope. Size-35 runner catalog values: dynamic C = 59.6 kN, static C0 = 89.5 kN, moment capacities Mx = 1.12 kN*m, My = 1.12 kN*m, Mz = 1.21 kN*m.

Envelope: single EN AW-6082-T6 aluminum plate 400 mm (along travel) x 250 mm (across rails) x 30 mm nominal. Two rails run parallel at 200 mm center-to-center; the plate is bolted to four runner blocks (two per rail) with M8 grade 8.8 screws on the runner's 72 x 40 mm hole pattern (16 bolts total). Payload attaches via a vertical arm whose tip is 150 mm above the subframe upper surface and 75 mm forward of the rail centerline midplane.

Service load at the payload tip.
- Vertical 3,000 N downward.
- Horizontal (cross-rail) 1,500 N.
- Dynamic accelerations up to 5 m/s^2 in the travel direction (inertial load 5 * combined payload + subframe mass).

Pass criteria include: per-runner dynamic P <= 0.5 * C = 29.8 kN with L10h >= 10,000 hours at 0.5 m/s mean speed; static S0 >= 4; catalog combined-load interaction <= 1.0; payload-tip deflection under service load <= 0.1 mm; first natural frequency >= 80 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: L10h = (C/P)^3 * (100 km / s * 2 * stroke * freq) is a closed-form catalog formula.

prompt.title: Aluminum subframe on dual size-35 ball rails
prompt.brief: Design an EN AW-6082-T6 subframe mounted on 4x size-35 ball-rail runner blocks, carrying a cantilever payload. Pass catalog combined-load interaction and positioning-stiffness targets.

load_cases.name: service_static
load_cases.application: at payload tip, 150 mm above + 75 mm forward of rail centerline midplane

boundary_conditions.rails: considered fixed reference; runner-to-rail interaction modeled by catalog stiffness
boundary_conditions.runner_bolt_pattern: bolts fully constrained at 4x runner block footprints

objective.minimize: subframe mass subject to stiffness + life constraints

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: P <= C/2 for L10h >= 10,000 h target at 0.5 m/s mean (catalog practice)
C0 / 4 with static SF S0 >= 4 per catalog
positioning stiffness typical for size-35 precision applications
isolation ratio >=4 over 20 Hz servo bandwidth

notes.numeric_source: Industrial ball-rail catalog 2024, size-35 full-length runner block: C=59.6 kN, C0=89.5 kN, Mx=My=1.12 kNm, Mz=1.21 kNm.
